mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in nonfarm payrolls PAYEMS signals weakening labor demand and rising job losses, which erodes consumer income and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward Sharpe ratio in months with declining payrolls is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.10) or Rank IC falls below -0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate UNRATE rises above 4.5%, it indicates that the economy has entered a recessionary phase with significant labor market slack, which depresses aggregate demand and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return after UNRATE crosses above 4.5% is not statistically below zero (one-sided t-test p>0.05) over a 10-year period, or if the signal generates negative mean return in at least 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production INDPRO and elevated federal funds rate FEDFUNDS creates a double blow: falling output reduces earnings expectations while tight monetary policy raises discount rates, compounding negative pressure on equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the joint signal from declining INDPRO and elevated FEDFUNDS does not produce a negative mean 20-day forward return (t-test p>0.05) or if the interaction term in a linear model has a coefficient not significantly different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During the financial crisis regime characterized by credit freeze and systemic risk, a negative quarterly GDPC1 growth reading confirms the depth of the recession, leading investors to sell equities as earnings revisions turn sharply negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward S&P 500 return following a negative GDPC1 quarter in the financial crisis period is not negative with statistical significance (p<0.05) or if more than 30% of such instances yield positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential decline in the federal funds rate FEDFUNDS signals that the central bank is easing monetary policy in response to economic weakness, which reduces the discount rate on equity cash flows and boosts investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return following a month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10) or if the Rank IC of the FEDFUNDS change is below +0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts HOUST fall below 1,200 thousand units, it signals a severe housing market downturn that drags down construction spending, household wealth, and consumer confidence, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the mean 60-day forward return after HOUST drops below 1200 is not statistically negative (one-tailed t-test p>0.05) across all historical occurrences, or if at least 25% of such episodes produce positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation CPIAUCSL combined with elevated federal funds rate FEDFUNDS traps the economy in a stagflation scenario: rising prices erode purchasing power while tight policy stifles growth, leading to negative equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the joint high-CPIAUCSL and high-FEDFUNDS signal does not yield a significantly negative mean 20-day forward return (t-test p<0.05) or if the interaction term in a regression has a coefficient of the wrong sign or zero.

mechanism_narrative: During economic recession, a decline in real retail sales RSAFS signals weakening consumer spending, which makes up the bulk of aggregate demand; this contraction directly harms corporate revenues and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return after a month-over-month decline in RSAFS during NBER-dated recessions is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) or if the mean return is positive in more than 20% of such instances.
